page: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Core Processor based on the Haswell architecture (x86_64). It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz without hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 30.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core, alongside a local 200 GB SSD. Virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 25% tier, reflecting the older Haswell architecture. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory performance shows a similar split: cached read bandwidth is average at 664.72 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block write bandwidth is poor, landing in the bottom 10% at 12.29 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the b2-30 offers dedicated physical resources and balanced memory-to-vCPU density without the premium of modern high-frequency architectures. This makes it a viable option for workloads that benefit from dedicated cores but do not require peak single-thread performance. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis database operations, and software compilation, where multi-core scaling can offset the weaker single-core throughput.
description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Haswell physical cores (x86_64) running at 2.0 GHz with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of RAM, a local 200 GB SSD, and runs on the KVM hypervisor. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core capabilities and average multi-core scaling, with Passmark and Geekbench multi-core scores landing in the middle tier. Cached memory bandwidth is average at 664.72 GB/sec, though uncached write speeds are poor. This resource profile offers balanced memory-to-core density, making it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for multi-threaded workloads that do not demand high single-thread frequency. It is best suited for static web serving, Redis key-value stores, software compilation, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Haswell cores, 30 GB of RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose KVM server with 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, offering stable multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core server with balanced memory and local SSD storage for stable, general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Haswell physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
30.0 GB of RAM paired with a local 200 GB SSD
Average multi-core performance and cached memory bandwidth
KVM hypervisor with one complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for static web serving, Redis, and multi-threaded applications
